meta.title: Veo 3.1 Video API | Native Audio, Text-to-Video, Frame Control & Reference Images | APIDot
meta.description: Access Google DeepMind Veo 3.1 on APIDot for 8-second video generation with native audio, reference images, start/end frame control, and Fast, Lite, or Quality pricing tiers.

hero.title: Veo 3.1 Video API Playground
hero.description: Run Veo 3.1 text-to-video, frame-guided video generation, and reference image workflows from one APIDot page across Lite, Fast, and Quality. Use the same async API pattern to test prompts, compare modes, and move the workflow into production without changing how your integration works.

overview.title: Use {modelName} when you need short-form video that carries sound, camera intent, and visual continuity at the same time
paragraphs: Veo 3.1 is a strong fit when you need a video API for short ads, product videos, ecommerce launches, storyboard tests, or social clips that should feel closer to a finished piece than a rough motion draft. The main value is not just text-to-video generation. It is that native audio, cinematic prompts, camera direction, and scene continuity can stay in the same workflow instead of being patched together afterward.

On APIDot, Veo 3.1 is available through Fast, Lite, and Quality variants for 8-second jobs with 16:9 or 9:16 output, reference-image workflows, and async delivery. The currently selected variant is {activeModelId}, billed at {priceLabel}.

A simple way to choose the right mode is this: stay with text-only when you are exploring ideas fast, switch to frame-guided video generation when you want to steer transitions or motion between shots, and use reference images when character, product, or brand consistency matters more than raw speed. That makes {modelName} especially useful for short-form marketing videos, storyboard work, previs, and other production-minded clips where control matters as much as motion.

capabilities.title: Key Veo 3.1 Video API capabilities teams actually use

items.title: Native audio video generation for short-form content
items.description: Veo 3.1 can generate dialogue, ambience, and sound effects with the video, which is why teams search for it when they need more than silent motion tests. On APIDot, that makes the first output much closer to something you can review for pacing, mood, and story rather than just visual movement.

items.title: Text-to-video for fast ideation and early prompt testing
items.description: If you want the fastest way to test a concept, text-to-video is still the cleanest starting point. `veo3.1-lite` is the text-only option for low-cost ideation, while `veo3.1-fast` and `veo3.1-quality` let teams stay in the same API surface when they want to move from loose concepts into more directed shots.

items.title: Frame-guided video generation for shot transitions
items.description: Frame mode is the practical choice when a prompt alone cannot hold the motion you need. APIDot exposes Veo 3.1 frame-guided generation through `input.generate_type: "frame"`, which lets you work from a first frame or from first-and-last-frame guidance for cleaner transitions, product reveals, and storyboard-style motion.

items.title: Reference image video generation for visual consistency
items.description: Reference image workflows are useful when the subject, style, or brand look needs to stay tighter across generations. On APIDot, reference mode is available only on `veo3.1-fast`, which makes it the best choice when you are generating marketing videos, character-led clips, or product scenes that need stronger visual consistency.

items.title: Cinematic prompts and camera control that translate better
items.description: Prompts that mention tracking shots, overhead framing, portrait composition, lighting cues, motion pacing, and atmosphere usually land better here than they do in generic video models. That is why creative teams often use Veo 3.1 for clips where camera intent matters, not just for scenes that need to move.

items.title: Lite vs Fast vs Quality is a workflow choice, not just a tier list
items.description: Use Lite when the goal is cheap text-only exploration, Fast when you want the most flexible working mode with frame and reference image support, and Quality when the final output matters more than turnaround. This split makes the Veo 3.1 API easier to adopt because teams can map the model choice to a clear production stage.

useCases.title: Common Veo 3.1 Video API use cases on APIDot
useCases.description: This page is designed for teams that already know they need short-form AI video and want a more search-friendly answer to the real operational questions: which Veo 3.1 variant to choose, when to use text-only versus frame or reference image mode, and how to plug the result into an async API workflow.
items: Short ad videos for product launches and paid campaigns
Product demo videos and ecommerce launch clips
Social media short videos with stronger pacing and shot direction
Storyboard tests and film previs clips
Game teaser videos and concept reveal clips
Training, explainer, and internal comms videos

quickstart.title: Start using {modelName} on APIDot in three steps

steps.title: Create your API key
steps.description: Generate an APIDot API key once and use it as the Bearer token for all Veo 3.1 Fast, Lite, and Quality requests.

steps.title: Top up credits
steps.description: Top up credits based on the Veo 3.1 variant and resolution you plan to run. Fast is optimized for iteration, Lite for cost efficiency, and Quality for premium output.

steps.title: Submit async jobs and retrieve results
steps.description: POST to {endpoint} with model="{activeModelId}", get a task ID back, then poll status or use callbacks to collect the finished video when the job completes.

items.question: What is Veo 3.1 Video API on APIDot?
items.answer: It is APIDot's unified entry point for `veo3.1-lite`, `veo3.1-fast`, and `veo3.1-quality`. You can test Veo 3.1 text-to-video, frame-guided generation, and supported reference image workflows in one place, then keep the same async integration pattern when you move the workload into production.

items.question: Which inputs, generation modes, and output settings are supported?
items.answer: The current page is built around fixed 8-second generation with `16:9` and `9:16` output, plus `720p`, `1080p`, or `4k` depending on the variant. `veo3.1-lite` is text-to-video only. `veo3.1-fast` supports text, frame-guided generation, and reference images. `veo3.1-quality` supports text plus frame-guided generation, but not reference mode.

items.question: Can I use reference images with every Veo 3.1 variant?
items.answer: No. Reference image video generation is available only on `veo3.1-fast`. If you want the lowest-cost way to test a text idea, use `veo3.1-lite`. If you want higher-fidelity frame-guided generation for a more polished clip, `veo3.1-quality` is the better fit, but it does not support reference mode.

items.question: When should I use frame mode instead of reference mode?
items.answer: Use frame mode when you already know the opening shot, the closing shot, or the transition you want to control. Use reference mode when the bigger goal is keeping the subject, product, or visual style consistent across generations. On APIDot, frame mode works on `veo3.1-fast` and `veo3.1-quality`, while reference mode is limited to `veo3.1-fast`.

items.question: Does Veo 3.1 generate native audio, and do failed jobs get charged?
items.answer: Yes. Native audio is one of the headline strengths of the Veo 3.1 family. On APIDot, credits are intended for successful generations rather than failed jobs.

items.question: Does the API use an async job pattern?
items.answer: Yes. You submit the request, receive a task ID, and then either poll the status endpoint or wait for a callback. That makes Veo 3.1 easier to plug into queue-based systems, content pipelines, and other production workflows without blocking the request lifecycle.

items.question: How do teams choose between Lite, Fast, and Quality?
items.answer: A practical rule is: use Lite for cheap text-only exploration, Fast when you need the broadest working mode with image guidance and frequent iteration, and Quality when the final video matters more than turnaround. Teams often move through them in that same order as a project becomes more defined.

items.question: What kinds of prompts and camera directions work best with Veo 3.1?
items.answer: The safest pattern is to be explicit about subject, action, camera perspective, movement, lighting, atmosphere, and audio intent. Prompts that use film language such as tracking shot, overhead shot, close-up, slow push-in, backlight, or ambient street sound tend to give Veo 3.1 clearer instructions than short generic prompts.
